hubs.name: Anchorage
hubs.role: Main air gateway; most trips start here
hubs.note: Rental cars, Alaska Railroad hub, road access to Kenai + Interior.

hubs.name: Fairbanks
hubs.note: Start of the Dalton; Chena Hot Springs; winter aurora capital.

hubs.name: Juneau
hubs.role: Capital; Southeast hub — no road out
hubs.note: Reached only by air or ferry. Mendenhall, whales, icefield.

hubs.name: Ketchikan
hubs.role: First Inside Passage port; totems + Misty Fjords
hubs.note: Air/ferry only. Big cruise stop.

hubs.name: Sitka
hubs.role: Russian-America history on the outer coast
hubs.note: Air/ferry only.

hubs.name: Skagway
hubs.role: Gold-rush railroad town; road to Yukon
hubs.note: Road-connected via Klondike Hwy through Canada; also ferry.

hubs.name: Haines
hubs.note: Road to Haines Junction, Yukon; short ferry to Skagway.

hubs.name: Seward
hubs.note: 2.5h drive or train from Anchorage.

hubs.name: Homer
hubs.role: End of the road; halibut + Kachemak Bay
hubs.note: 4.5h drive from Anchorage down the Sterling Hwy.

hubs.name: Whittier
hubs.role: Prince William Sound access through the tunnel
hubs.note: One-lane tunnel on a schedule; 1.5h from Anchorage.

hubs.name: Valdez
hubs.note: Spectacular 5.5h drive from Anchorage over Thompson Pass.

hubs.name: Talkeetna
hubs.note: 2.5h from Anchorage; train stop.

hubs.name: Denali Park / Healy
hubs.note: 4h from Anchorage, 2h from Fairbanks; on rail + Parks Hwy.

hubs.name: Glennallen
hubs.role: Copper River crossroads for Wrangell-St. Elias
hubs.note: Junction of Glenn + Richardson Hwys.

hubs.name: McCarthy / Kennecott
hubs.note: 60mi gravel McCarthy Rd; footbridge to town.

hubs.name: Tok
hubs.note: Junction for the Alaska Hwy and Glenn Hwy/Tok Cutoff.

hubs.name: Delta Junction
hubs.note: Richardson Hwy to Fairbanks or Valdez.

hubs.name: Coldfoot
hubs.note: Fuel, food, beds at Dalton Mile 175. Nothing for 240mi north.

hubs.name: Deadhorse / Prudhoe Bay
hubs.role: End of the Dalton at the Arctic Ocean
hubs.note: Oil camp; Arctic Ocean tour must be pre-booked.

hubs.name: Nome
hubs.role: Iditarod finish; roads to nowhere; gold beaches
hubs.note: Fly-in only; 3 gravel roads for wildlife + birding.

hubs.name: Kotzebue
hubs.role: Arctic hub for Kobuk Valley + Noatak
hubs.note: Fly-in only; above the Arctic Circle.

hubs.name: Utqiagvik (Barrow)
hubs.note: Fly-in only; polar night + midnight sun extremes.

hubs.name: Kodiak
hubs.note: Air or a long ferry from Homer/Whittier.

hubs.name: Dutch Harbor / Unalaska
hubs.role: Bering Sea crab port; WWII battlefield
hubs.note: Fly-in or the multi-day Aleutian ferry.

hubs.name: King Salmon
hubs.note: Jet from Anchorage, then float plane to Brooks Camp.

hubs.name: Cordova
hubs.note: Air or ferry only; no road connection.

hubs.name: Gustavus
hubs.note: Air or ferry; short road to Bartlett Cove.

drives.note: Turnagain Arm the whole way; one of the great drives.
One-lane tunnel runs on a schedule; check times.
Fuel + food in Soldotna and Ninilchik.
Denali View South pullout at Mile 135.
Full day; break at Denali or Talkeetna.
Matanuska Glacier, Thompson Pass, waterfalls.
Matanuska Glacier + Sheep Mountain en route.
Nenana river-town stop.
Via Cooper Landing + the Russian River.
Gravel; Arctic Circle sign at Mile 115. Carry 2 spares.
No services. Atigun Pass over the Brooks Range.
Paved; aurora + Ice Museum at the end.
Wrangell peaks come into view.
Thompson Pass + Keystone Canyon waterfalls.
Last 60mi are slow gravel; ~3h alone.
15 min by ferry vs 7.5h by road around the mountains.
The sane way between the two.
No road to Juneau — fly or take the ferry.
Ferry is ~9h; flight 35 min.
For Glacier Bay.
Or ~9-12h ferry from Homer/Whittier.
Then a float plane to Brooks Camp for the bears.
No road to the rest of Alaska.
Overnight ferry across Shelikof Strait.
Passes Columbia Glacier country.
Cordova has no road link at all.
The 1.5-day sailing up the Inside Passage from Washington.
